lines.content: This is a project template for a greenfield Java project. It's named after the Java mascot _Duke_. Given below are instructions on how to use it.
Prerequisites: JDK 17 (use the exact version), update Intellij to the most recent version.

1. **Ensure Intellij JDK 17 is defined as an SDK**, as described [here](https://www.jetbrains.com/help/idea/sdk.html#set-up-jdk) -- this step is not needed if you have used JDK 17 in a previous Intellij project.
1. **Import the project _as a Gradle project_**, as described [here](https://se-education.org/guides/tutorials/intellijImportGradleProject.html).

1. **Verify the setup**: After the importing is complete, locate the `src/main/java/seedu/duke/Duke.java` file, right-click it, and choose `Run Duke.main()`. If the setup is correct, you should see something like the below:
Type some word and press enter to let the execution proceed to the end.

**Warning:** Keep the `src\main\java` folder as the root folder for Java files (i.e., don't rename those folders or move Java files to another folder outside of this folder path), as this is the default location some tools (e.g., Gradle) expect to find Java files.
* This project uses Gradle for build automation and dependency management. It includes a basic build script as well (i.e. the `build.gradle` file).
* If you are new to Gradle, refer to the [Gradle Tutorial at se-education.org/guides](https://se-education.org/guides/tutorials/gradle.html).
* To run _I/O redirection_ tests (aka _Text UI tests_), navigate to the `text-ui-test` and run the `runtest(.bat/.sh)` script.
* A skeleton JUnit test (`src/test/java/seedu/duke/DukeTest.java`) is provided with this project template.
* If you are new to JUnit, refer to the [JUnit Tutorial at se-education.org/guides](https://se-education.org/guides/tutorials/junit.html).
* A sample CheckStyle rule configuration is provided in this project.
* If you are new to Checkstyle, refer to the [Checkstyle Tutorial at se-education.org/guides](https://se-education.org/guides/tutorials/checkstyle.html).

The project uses [GitHub actions](https://github.com/features/actions) for CI. When you push a commit to this repo or PR against it, GitHub actions will run automatically to build and verify the code as updated by the commit/PR.
`/docs` folder contains a skeleton version of the project documentation.
Steps for publishing documentation to the public:
1. If you are using this project template for an individual project, go your fork on GitHub.
If you are using this project template for a team project, go to the team fork on GitHub.
1. Scroll down to the `GitHub Pages` section.
1. Optionally, use the `choose a theme` button to choose a theme for your documentation.
* Main entry-point for the java.classmate.Classmate application.
System.out.println("I'm your CEG course planning assistant!");
* Prints the list of ClassMate commands and their descriptions
* to help users on how to use the application
System.out.println("- Show prerequisites for a module");
System.out.println("- Show information for a module");
System.out.println("- Check if module is available in a specific semester");
System.out.println("- Show detailed info for a specialisation");
* Represents a custom exception used in ClassMate application.
* Thrown when invalid input or application-specific errors happen.
* Represents a use command parsed from input.
* Stores the command word and corresponding arguments.
* Constructs a {@code Command} with the specified command word and arguments.
* @param commandWord The main command keyword entered by the user.
* @param args The arguments associated with the command.
* Returns the command word of this command.
* Returns the arguments associated with the command.
* @return The command arguments as a string.
* Stores and manages a list of core modules required to fulfil the major.
/** Store major requirements as a list of Module objects */
* Constructs a {@code Major} object and initializes list
* of core modules required for CEG major.
* Returns a formatted string containing the list of core modules
* required to fulfil the CEG major.
* @return A string listing all core modules.
return "Core Modules List is currently empty.";
String moduleListHeader = "Here is a list of modules required to fulfill CEG Major: ";
* Initializes the list of core modules and their prerequisites for CEG major.
Module ma1512 = new Module("MA1512", "Differential Equations for Engineering");
Module ma1508e = new Module("MA1508E", "Linear Algebra for Engineering");
Module cg1111a = new Module("CG1111A", "Engineering Principles and Practice I");
Module cg2111a = new Module("CG2111A", "Engineering Principles and Practice II");
Module cg2027 = new Module("CG2027", "Transistor-level Digital Circuit");
Module cs2040c = new Module("CS2040C", "Data Structures and Algorithms");
Module cs2113 = new Module("CS2113", "Software Engineering & Object-Oriented Programming");
Module cs2107 = new Module("CS2107", "Introduction to Information Security");
Module cg2271 = new Module("CG2271", "Real-time Operating System");
Module cg3201 = new Module("CG3201", "Machine Learning and Deep Learning");
* Represents a university module consisting of a module code,
* module name, and a list of prerequisite module(s).
* Constructs a {@code Module} with the specified module code and name.
* @param moduleCode The code of the module (e.g., CS2113).
* @param moduleName The title of the module.
* @throws ClassMateException If the module code or name is empty.
// Add guard clause against empty details
throw new ClassMateException("Module details cannot be empty.");
* Adds a prerequisite module code to this module.
* Duplicate prerequisites will not be added.
* @param prereqCode The module code of the prerequisite module.
// Add guard clause against duplicate prerequisite entry: See ModuleTest class
* Returns a string representation of the module.
* @return A string containing the module code and module title.
* Returns the list of prerequisite module codes.
* @return An {@code ArrayList} containing prerequisite module codes.
* Returns the list of prerequisite module codes.
* @return An {@code ArrayList} containing prerequisite module codes.
* Parses user input into a {@code Command} object.
* The parser separates the command word from its arguments (e.g. module code / specialisation name)
* so that the main program can execute the command accordingly
* Parses the given user input and returns the corresponding {@code Command}.
* @return A {@code Command} object containing the command word and its arguments.
throw new ClassMateException("Please enter a non-empty input!");
* Contains unit tests for the Module class.
* Tests for empty module codes and whether all modules are unique.
Module module = new Module("CG1111A", "Engineering Principles and Practice I");
* Since prerequisites are stored as an ArrayList<String>,
* Size of the ArrayList will increment without detecting duplicate entry
assertEquals(1, module.getPrerequisites().size(), "Prerequisite module should not be duplicated.");
* Throw custom exception for null string input
assertEquals("Module details cannot be empty.", e.getMessage());
* Contains unit tests for the Parser class.
* Tests whether user input is correctly parsed into Command objects,
* such as valid commands and invalid or empty inputs.
